message.reasoning_content:
We need to produce JSON for stage web-002-backlink: create webpage 2 and link it back to page 1 without breaking page 1. Existing files: site/index.html (page 1). We need to create site/page-2.html that links back to page 1 (index.html). Also we must not break page 1, so we should not modify index.html. So only new file: site/page-2.html. Content: Should be a valid HTML page, with a link back to index.html. Probably something like: Page 2
This is page 2. Back to Home
We need to ensure it's valid HTML. Also we might want to keep similar structure as index.html: nav empty, main content. We must output JSON with files array containing that file. Notes: short note. Let's craft JSON: { "files": [ { "path": "site/page-2.html", "content": "\n\n\n \n Page 2\n\n\n
\n
\n This is page 2.\n Back to Home\n
\n\n" } ], "notes": "Created page-2.html with link back to index.html." } Make sure no extra text. Ensure proper escaping of double quotes inside JSON string: we need to escape them. In the content string, we have double quotes around attribute values. In JSON we need to escape them as \". So we need to produce a string where the double quotes are escaped. Let's write the content exactly as we want, but with escaped quotes. Original content: Page 2
This is page 2. Back to Home
We need to replace each " with \". Also we need to keep newlines as actual newlines inside JSON string (allowed). We'll produce: "\n\n\n \n Page 2\n\n\n
\n
\n This is page 2.\n Back to Home\n
\n\n" Check: we have escaped the double quotes after lang=, charset=, title=, href=. Now produce JSON. Let's output.
